overrides.notes: Pea puffers are territorial fin-nippers and bettas are slow, long-finned, territorial fish. This pairing is strongly discouraged.
Otocinclus are one of the more defensible pea puffer tankmates, but only in mature, heavily planted setups with careful monitoring.
Adult angelfish can eat small slender tetras such as neons, especially once the angelfish is mature.
Tiger barbs are active fin-nippers and bettas are slow long-finned fish. This should be treated as a high-risk caution pairing, not a normal community match.
Both species defend similar bottom territories. They may coexist only in large, structured aquariums and are a poor fit for most community tanks.
Oscars are predatory cichlids and neon tetras are small prey-sized fish.
Serpae tetras can nip fins and angelfish are slow, long-finned cichlids. This is a caution pairing even when water parameters overlap.
